system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          0.0025;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|k|omega|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    "(U|k|omega|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    2;
    nCorrectors         1;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;

laminar
{
    laminarModel    generalizedNewtonian;

    generalizedNewtonianCoeffs
    {
        viscosityModel  CrossPowerLaw;

        nuInf   10;
        m       0.4;
        n       3;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";

}

viscosityModel  constant;

nu              1e-02;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

boundaryField

Cannot find file "points" in directory "polyMesh" in times "0" down to constant

    From function virtual Foam::IOobject Foam::fileOperation::findInstance(const Foam::IOobject&, Foam::scalar, const Foam::word&) const
    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': "ERROR: 
wrong token type - expected word, found on line 66 the punctuation token '{'

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_C_20260516_195157_run1/runs/Cylinder/3/system/blockMeshDict/edges at line 66.

    From function Foam::Istream& Foam::operator>>(Istream&, word&)
    in file primitives/strings/word/wordIO.C at line 74.

FOAM exiting"}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Token Type Mismatch

**Error Message:**
```
wrong token type - expected word, found on line 66 the punctuation token '{'
```

**Diagnosis:**

Looking at the current `blockMeshDict`, the `edges` section contains full sub-dictionaries enclosed in `{}` blocks, but the parser is seeing a `{` token where it expects a `word`. This suggests the OpenFOAM version being used does **not** support the sub-dictionary format for `edges` — it expects the older style where each edge is specified as a simple keyword followed by parameters.

The current `edges` section looks like:
```
edges
(
    {
        arc 16 17;
        radius 0.666666666666667;
        startAngles (0 -180);
        endAngles (0 -0);
    }
    { ... }
    ...
);
```

But the parser expects something like:
```
edges
(
    arc 16 17 (0.33 -0.67 -0.5)
        radius 0.666666666666667
        startAngles (0 -180)
        endAngles (0 -0);
    arc 24 25 (0.33 0.67 -0.5)
        radius 0.666666666666667
        startAngles (0 -180)
        endAngles (0 -0);
    ...
);
```

That is, **no curly braces** wrapping each edge definition — just the edge keyword followed directly by the parameters.

## Error 2: `log.pimpleFoam` — Cascading Failure

**Diagnosis:**

Since `blockMesh` failed, no mesh was generated, so `pimpleFoam` cannot find the mesh files (`points`, `faces`, etc.).

**Resolution:**

Fix the `edges` section in `system/blockMeshDict`:

1. **Remove the curly braces `{}`** from around each edge definition. Each edge should appear as a bare entry starting with the curve type keyword (`arc`, `line`, etc.), followed by the vertex indices and parameters.

2. The correct format should be:
```
edges
(
    arc 16 17 (0.33 -0.67 -0.5)
        radius 0.666666666666667
        startAngles (0 -180)
        endAngles (0 -0);
    arc 24 25 (0.33 0.67 -0.5)
        radius 0.666666666666667
        startAngles (0 -180)
        endAngles (0 -0);
    arc 17 18 (1 -0.67 -0.5)
        radius 0.666666666666667
        startAngles (0 -180)
        endAngles (0 -0);
    ... (continue for all 12 edges)
);
```

3. **Verify vertex ordering consistency.** Check that the arc endpoints follow a consistent convention (e.g., always going counter-clockwise around the intended cylinder). For the offset cylinder case, ensure the arc sequence properly traces the outer boundary of the obstacle.

4. **Ensure all 12 arc entries are present** and none reference non-existent vertices.

5. After making these changes, run `blockMesh` first, then `pimpleFoam`.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'In the edges section, remove the curly braces {} wrapper from each individual edge definition so that each edge appears as a bare entry starting with the curve keyword; Replace the first edge entry from { arc 16 17; radius 0.666666666666667; startAngles (0 -180); endAngles (0 -0); } to arc 16 17 (0.33 -0.67 -0.5) radius 0.666666666666667 startAngles (0 -180) endAngles (0 -0); Apply the same fix to all 12 edge entries removing their surrounding curly braces and adding the vertex coordinate after the vertex index arguments'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a laminar offsetcylinder simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm with a generalized Newtonian model and Cross-Power-Law viscosity for momentum transport, characterized by specific parameters nuInf=10, m=0.4, n=3. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 1.5m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls, including the cylinder surface. Use timestep of 0.0025 and output every 0.05. Finaltime is 0.5. use constant viscosity model with nu value of 1e-02 in physical properties.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
